solution.rawText:
R4                                            R
We have   2 f(x) dx = A, and the question asks about        f(x + 2) dx between various limits.
The graph of y = f(x + 2) is the graph of y = f(x) translated left by 2 units, so we can draw a
sketch to see what effect this has on the area between x = 2 and x = 4:

               y                                                   y




                                           x                                                      x
               0        2         4                                0         2       4



                   R4            R2
This means that    2 f(x) dx =   0 f(x + 2) dx = A
(If you have learnt about integration by substitution, youR can also show this as follows: writing
                                            R4              2
x = u + 2 gives dx = du, and u = x − 2, so 2 f(x) dx = 0 f(u + 2) du)
Therefore the answer is either A or B. We have
                        Z 2                     Z 2               Z 2
                            [f(x + 2) + 1] dx =     f(x + 2) dx +     1 dx
                            0                    0                     0
                                                       R2
The first integral equals A, as we have shown, and      0 1 dx = 2, so the whole integral equals
A + 2 and the correct answer is B.
